principle: Ordinary outputs should expose one primary next action and keep diagnostics, history, and inventories behind selectors or verbose detail unless they change that action.

command_roles.primary_next_action: inspect changed paths and selected proof
inspect active planning only when active work matters
route to current warning, action, or section
inspect static policy only by section
inspect resolved config only when posture matters
preserve CLI next_safe_action when CLI is unavailable

warning_roles.preferred_remedy: prefer Planning CLI state writers and keep checker pressure until legacy TODO shapes disappear
route agents to a single active item through summary/start instead of exposing broad active queues
require promotion/new-plan commands to create linked execplan surfaces
make next_safe_action explicit about when planning ownership is required
keep diagnostic for stale or external/manual surface references
prefer schema-backed execplan scaffolds and command-time validation
refuse or normalize multiple active milestones in writer commands
keep next action in canonical_core and generated projections synchronized
make readiness values command-owned and schema-validated
demote execution chronology to archive/detail unless it changes continuation
remove notebook-like residue from first-line execplans
improve scaffolds or writer prompts so required proof/scope fields are filled earlier
move ordinary closeout to a single command-owned writer
keep active planning state narrow and route closed work to archives
make selected proof and achieved proof explicit in writer/closeout commands
route intent satisfaction through closeout/intent-verification affordances
keep diagnostic for schema drift in checked-in planning records
keep diagnostic for external/manual evidence drift
route foreign project-management shapes through import/promotion helpers before use
constrain Planning state activation shapes in CLI writers
use decomposition records for epic-shaped work instead of freehand artifacts
route decomposition creation through package-owned paths
route common freehand artifact intake to focused follow-up #1033 before demoting this warning
keep roadmap ideas out of active execution state until promoted
require promotion triggers for candidate work
require reopen triggers for retained historical/candidate work
demote stale candidates from first-line context and route by explicit review
make promotion commands preserve source item and execplan linkage
keep diagnostic for generated/fallback startup guidance drift
keep diagnostic for workflow fallback projection drift
keep diagnostic for documentation role/authority drift
keep diagnostic for generated docs that drift from source
keep archive volume behind selectors and route reusable residue out of archives
route durable knowledge to Memory and active/future work to Planning
keep as recovery-only drift diagnostic; routine command-owned mutations should prove validity through schema, command output, and normal git diff rather than manual ledger repair
retain as archive/manual-drift diagnostic; ordinary active-run completion is now owned by planning closeout evidence options from #1029
keep diagnostic for source/payload mismatch
demote from first-line context and clean through lifecycle commands

provenance_policy.normal_operation_rule: Normal Planning CLI mutations may update this surface while the transition is active, but callers should not treat the ledger as the proof that a routine mutation is legitimate; schema validation, command output, and git diff are the ordinary evidence.
provenance_policy.recovery_rule: record-recovery is the expected explicit writer for emergency/manual repair and should carry the reason for the exceptional mutation.
provenance_policy.warning_scope: planning_manual_mutation_unstamped is scoped to external/manual dirty planning surfaces, not to routine command-created state that has already passed command-time validation.
provenance_policy.exit_condition: When common Planning state edits have command-owned writers and checker warnings point only at external/manual drift, this surface can be reduced to recovery-only detail or removed from normal-operation expectations.

retained_diagnostics.trigger: dirty managed Planning surfaces whose current hash is not covered by explicit recovery provenance
checked-in Planning records that cannot validate against their canonical schema

routed_followups.reason: freehand durable Planning artifacts still need a command-owned intake helper before the warning can demote to external/manual drift only

source_boundary_review.closure_rule: #1021 should remain open until remaining source_boundary_fix_candidate and missing_helper roles are either command-owned, demoted with evidence, or routed to focused child issues.

closeout_writer_direction.interim_owner: planning closeout --proof-from <proof> plus finish-run evidence options
